_metadata.note: All scores are illustrative baselines under doctoral validation. Panel invited to challenge and refine.

arctic_weights._note: CD weight reflects inaugural session identification of cognitive operations as critical High North adversarial vector

norway.name: Norway
norway.cd_notes: Emerging cognitive warfare doctrine. Maskirovka classification speed: ~2.5hrs in Inject I-6.
norway.primary_seam: Svalbard sovereignty ambiguity — Norwegian operational transparency

finland.name: Finland
finland.cd_notes: Strongest allied CD score. New NATO integration creates CD-NOF-05 (Alliance Coherence) watch.
finland.primary_seam: New NATO integration — Article 5 commitment narrative exploitation

sweden.name: Sweden
sweden.cd_notes: Historical neutrality creates CD vulnerability to reactivation narratives.
sweden.primary_seam: New NATO integration — historical neutrality reactivation

denmark.name: Denmark/Greenland
denmark.cd_notes: CD emerging gap. US-Denmark-Greenland sovereignty dynamics create perception management vulnerability.

iceland.name: Iceland
iceland.cd_notes: CRITICAL: CD 38 = lowest allied score. 44-pt gap vs Russia (CD 82). Maskirovka operates below Iceland's classification threshold. CD-NOF-01 failure observed in Inject I-6.
iceland.primary_seam: CD Doctrine Asymmetry (44 pts vs Russia) + IIC gap (46 pts vs Russia) — compounding gray zone vulnerability

russia.name: Russia
russia.cd_notes: Institutionalized maskirovka doctrine. CD 82 reflects demonstrated strategic deception capability at operational and strategic scale. Primary cognitive warfare actor in High North.
russia.primary_seam: N/A — adversarial actor exploiting seams in allied governance architecture

governance_seams.label: CD Doctrine Asymmetry
governance_seams.description: Russian maskirovka calibrated to operate below Iceland's cognitive classification threshold (CD: 38) while Norway (CD: 61) has already recognized the operation as adversarial — creating a 2.5-hour alliance perception split during which coordinated response is impossible.
governance_seams.gray_zone_vector: Cognitive operation timed to exploit Iceland's classification delay. Norway and Iceland reach different threat assessments simultaneously — preventing Article 4 consultation activation.

governance_seams.label: Alliance Cognitive Coherence Gap
governance_seams.description: Differential deception-recognition frameworks between recently-integrated NATO members (Finland CD: 64, Sweden CD: 60) and Iceland (CD: 38) create an exploitable wedge in alliance threat perception coherence.
governance_seams.gray_zone_vector: Adversarial cognitive operation drives split in allied threat assessment at critical Article 4/5 decision moment — the gray zone between incident and armed attack.

governance_seams.label: ASI Governance Disparity
governance_seams.description: Adversarial autonomous ops calibrated to exploit Icelandic ASI governance gap while Norway governs response.
governance_seams.gray_zone_vector: The space between autonomous system encounter and authorized governance response — widened by Iceland's ASI gap vs Norway.

governance_seams.label: IIC Resilience Asymmetry
governance_seams.description: Russia IIC 88 vs Iceland IIC 42 — widest gap in allied actor set. Sustained information operations exploit Iceland's weakest IIC score.
governance_seams.gray_zone_vector: Narrative operations targeting NATO solidarity sequenced to hit Iceland first — the gray zone where adversarial narrative operates before Nordic counter-narrative governance activates.

governance_seams.label: Decision Tempo Gap
governance_seams.description: Norway NDM 80 vs Denmark/Greenland NDM 75. Hybrid operation requiring coordinated Norway-Greenland response within 6 hours finds Denmark/Greenland unable to keep pace.
governance_seams.gray_zone_vector: The coordination gray zone between Norway's fast inter-ministerial decision cycle and Denmark/Greenland's slower equivalent.
